pullRequest.title: DOM-75558 fix: fix error when deleting project tags [DOM-75558]

nodes.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Handle missing tag as a user-facing failure, not a 500**

Line 97 throws `IllegalStateException` when the tag is absent. In the `delete` flow (Line 91), that will bubble up as a server error for stale/racy requests instead of returning a flash error.



<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 def delete = DominoSecuredAction { implicit request =>
   implicit val context: DominoContext = request.dominoContext
   systemAuthorizationChecker.authorize(request.user, SystemOperation.ManageProjectTags)
   ControllerHelpers.handlePostRequest("id") { param =>
     val id = param("id")
     val tagId = new ObjectId(id)
     logger.info(s"Project tag deleted. tagId=$tagId user=${request.user.username}")
-    doDeleteTag(tagId)
-    Redirect(projecttags.routes.ProjectTagController.index()).flashing(StaticHelpers.FlashMessageKey -> "The tag was deleted.")
+    try {
+      doDeleteTag(tagId)
+      Redirect(projecttags.routes.ProjectTagController.index()).flashing(StaticHelpers.FlashMessageKey -> "The tag was deleted.")
+    } catch {
+      case UserErrorException(message, _) =>
+        Redirect(projecttags.routes.ProjectTagController.index()).flashing(StaticHelpers.FlashErrorMessageKey -> message)
+    }
   }
 }
 
 def doDeleteTag(tagId: ObjectId)(implicit context: DominoContext, request: SecuredRequest[_]): Unit = {
-  val tagName = projectTagManager.findOneById(tagId).getOrElse(throw new IllegalStateException("Tag not found")).name
+  val tagName = projectTagManager.findOneById(tagId).map(_.name).getOrElse(throw UserErrorException("Tag not found."))
   projectTagManager.delete(tagId)
   eventTracker.recordUserEvent(request.user.eventUser, DeleteTagEvent(tagId, tagName), request.clientSource)
 }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@server/app/domino/server/projecttags/ProjectTagController.scala` around lines
91 - 100, The delete flow currently lets doDeleteTag(tagId) throw
IllegalStateException when projectTagManager.findOneById(tagId) returns None;
change doDeleteTag to return an Either/Option or throw a domain-specific
exception and have the delete action handle the missing-tag case so it responds
with a user-facing flash message rather than a 500. Concretely, update
doDeleteTag(ObjectId) to return Option[(ObjectId, String)] or None when not
found (or throw a NotFoundException), and modify the delete action (the
controller method that calls doDeleteTag) to check the result and call
Redirect(...).flashing(StaticHelpers.FlashMessageKey -> "Tag not found or
already deleted.") on missing tags, otherwise proceed with delete and record the
event.
```

</details>
